Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n147\t        self._reload_manager = None\n148\t\n149\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n150\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n151\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n152\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n153\t        return self._zai_client\n154\t\n155\t    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n156\t        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n157\t        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n158\t            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n159\t            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n160\t            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n161\t            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n162\t        return self._router_zai_client\n163\t\n164\t    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n165\t        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n166\t        import hashlib\n167\t        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n168\t\n169\t    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n170\t        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n171\t        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n172\t        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n173\t\n174\t        if cache_key in _ROUTER_CACHE:\n175\t            classifications, timestamp = _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n176\t            age = time.time() - timestamp\n177\t            if age < _CACHE_TTL:\n178\t                logger.info(f\"Router cache HIT for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n179\t                return classifications\n180\t            else:\n181\t                # Remove expired entry\n182\t                del _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n183\t                logger.info(f\"Router cache EXPIRED for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]}\")\n184\t\n185\t        return None\n186\t\n187\t    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n188\t        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n189\t        # Prune cache if at capacity\n190\t        if len(_ROUTER_CACHE) >= _MAX_CACHE_SIZE:\n191\t            # Remove oldest entry (first in dict)\n192\t            oldest_key = next(iter(_ROUTER_CACHE))\n193\t            del _ROUTER_CACHE[oldest_key]\n194\t            logger.info(f\"Router cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n195\t\n196\t        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n197\t        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n198\t        _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key] = (classifications, time.time())\n199\t        logger.info(f\"Router cache STORED utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (cache size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n200\t\n201\t    async def _get_store(self):\n202\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n203\t        if self.store is None:\n204\t            self.store = get_store()\n205\t        return self.store\n206\t\n207\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n208\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n209\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n210\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n211\t        return self._reload_manager\n212\t\n213\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n214\t        \"\"\"\n215\t        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n216\t\n217\t        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n218\t        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n219\t        \"\"\"\n220\t        try:\n221\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n222\t        except Exception as e:\n223\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n224\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n225\t\n226\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n227\t        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n228\t\n229\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n230\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n231\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n232\t        \"\"\"\n233\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\n234\t\n235\t    async def classify_utterance(\n236\t        self,\n237\t        utterance: str,\n238\t        session_id: str,\n239\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n240\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n241\t        \"\"\"\n242\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n243\t\n244\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n245\t\n246\t        Args:\n247\t            utterance: The user utterance\n248\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n249\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n250\t\n251\t        Returns:\n252\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n253\t\n254\t        Raises:\n255\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n256\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n257\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n258\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n259\t        \"\"\"\n260\t        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n261\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n262\t        if cached_result is not None:\n263\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n264\t            empty_breakdown = {\n265\t                \"cached\": True,\n266\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n267\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n268\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n269\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n270\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n271\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n272\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n273\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n274\t            }\n275\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n276\t\n277\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n278\t        retry_count = 0\n279\t        raw_response = None\n280\t\n281\t        try:\n282\t            # Measure total classification time\n283\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n284\t\n285\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n286\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n287\t\n288\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n289\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n290\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n291\t\n292\t            # Build user message\n293\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n294\t\n295\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n296\t\n297\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n298\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n299\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n300\t\n301\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n302\t\n303\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n304\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n305\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n306\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n307\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n308\t                user_message=user_message,\n309\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n310\t                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n311\t                temperature=0.0,\n312\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n313\t            )\n314\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n315\t\n316\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n317\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n318\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n319\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n320\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n321\t\n322\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n323\t            raw_response = response\n324\t\n325\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n326\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n327\t            try:\n328\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n329\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n330\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n331\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n332\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n333\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n334\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n335\t                raise e\n336\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n337\t\n338\t            # Measure classification processing time\n339\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n340\t            classifications = []\n341\t\n342\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n343\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n344\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n345\t                try:\n346\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n347\t                except ValueError:\n348\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n349\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n350\t\n351\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n352\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n353\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n354\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n355\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n356\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n357\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n358\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n359\t                )\n360\t                classifications.append(classification)\n361\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n362\t\n363\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n364\t\n365\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n366\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n367\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n368\t            inference_str = f\"{timing_inference_ms:.2f}\" if timing_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n369\t            logger.info(\n370\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n371\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n372\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n373\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n374\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={inference_str} \"\n375\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n376\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n377\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n378\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n379\t            )\n380\t\n381\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n382\t\n383\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n384\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n385\t            timing_breakdown = {\n386\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n387\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n388\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n389\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(timing_inference_ms, 2) if timing_inference_ms is not None else None,\n390\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n391\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n392\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n393\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n394\t            }\n395\t\n396\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n397\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n398\t\n399\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n400\t\n401\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n402\t            \"\"\"\n403\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n404\t\n405\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n406\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n407\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n408\t            \"\"\"\n409\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n410\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n411\t\n412\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n413\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n414\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n415\t                retry_count += 1\n416\t                try:\n417\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n418\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n419\t                        utterance=utterance,\n420\t                        session_id=session_id,\n421\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n422\t                    )\n423\t                    return classifications, _\n424\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n425\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n426\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n427\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n428\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n429\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n430\t                    ) from retry_e\n431\t\n432\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n433\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n434\t                parse_error=str(e),\n435\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n436\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n437\t            ) from e\n438\t\n439\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n440\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n441\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n442\t\n443\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n444\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n445\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n446\t\n447\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n448\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n449\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n450\t\n451\t        except Exception as e:\n452\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n453\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n454\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n455\t\n456\t    async def route_utterance(\n457\t        self,\n458\t        utterance: str,\n459\t        utterance_id: str,\n460\t        session_id: str,\n461\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n462\t        \"\"\"\n463\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n464\t\n465\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n466\t\n467\t        Args:\n468\t            utterance: The user utterance\n469\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n470\t            session_id: Session ID\n471\t\n472\t        Returns:\n473\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n474\t\n475\t        Raises:\n476\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n477\t        \"\"\"\n478\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n479\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n480\t\n481\t        try:\n482\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n483\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n484\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n485\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n486\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n487\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n488\t\n489\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n490\t            try:\n491\t                store = await self._get_store()\n492\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n493\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n494\t            except Exception as e:\n495\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n496\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n497\t\n498\t            routed_intents = []\n499\t            for classification in classifications:\n500\t                # Create intent ID\n501\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n502\t\n503\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n504\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n505\t                    classification=classification,\n506\t                    session_id=session_id,\n507\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n508\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n509\t                )\n510\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n511\t\n512\t            return routed_intents\n513\t\n514\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n515\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n516\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n517\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n518\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n519\t                utterance=utterance,\n520\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n521\t                session_id=session_id,\n522\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n523\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n524\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n525\t            )\n526\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n527\t\n528\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n529\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n530\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n531\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n532\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n533\t                utterance=utterance,\n534\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n535\t                session_id=session_id,\n536\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n537\t            )\n538\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n539\t\n540\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n541\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n542\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n543\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n544\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n545\t                utterance=utterance,\n546\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n547\t                session_id=session_id,\n548\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n549\t            )\n550\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n551\t\n552\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n553\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n554\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n555\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n556\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n557\t                utterance=utterance,\n558\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n559\t                session_id=session_id,\n560\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n561\t            )\n562\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n563\t\n564\t    async def process_intent(\n565\t        self,\n566\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n567\t    ) -> dict:\n568\t        \"\"\"\n569\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n570\t\n571\t        Args:\n572\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n573\t\n574\t        Returns:\n575\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n576\t        \"\"\"\n577\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n578\t\n579\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n580\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n581\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n582\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n583\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n584\t        # the dispatch itself.\n585\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n586\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n587\t\n588\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n589\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n590\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n591\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n592\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n593\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n594\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n595\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n596\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n597\t            )\n598\t\n599\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n600\t            store = await self._get_store()\n601\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n602\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n603\t                \"resolved\",\n604\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n605\t            )\n606\t\n607\t            return {\n608\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n609\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n610\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n611\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n612\t            }\n613\t\n614\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n615\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n616\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n617\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n618\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n619\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n620\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n621\t            )\n622\t\n623\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n624\t            store = await self._get_store()\n625\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n626\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n627\t                \"resolved\",\n628\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n629\t            )\n630\t\n631\t            return {\n632\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n633\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n634\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n635\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n636\t            }\n637\t\n638\t        try:\n639\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n640\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n641\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n642\t            else:\n643\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n644\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n645\t        except Exception:\n646\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n647\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n648\t            raise\n649\t\n650\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n651\t        return result\n652\t\n653\t    async def _persist_timings(\n654\t        self,\n655\t        intent_id: str,\n656\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n657\t    ) -> None:\n658\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n659\t        try:\n660\t            store = await self._get_store()\n661\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n662\t        except Exception as e:\n663\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n664\t\n665\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n666\t        self,\n667\t )\n720\t\n721\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n722\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n723\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n724\t           # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n748\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n749\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n750\t\n751\t                # Increment completed count\n752\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n753\t\n754\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n755\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n756\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n757\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n758\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n759\t                    total=total_sources,\n760\t                    source_name=source.value,\n761\t                    source_status=result.status,\n762\t                ))\n763\t\n764\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n765\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n766\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n767\t                timings.record(\n768\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n769\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, failed_sources.append({\n784\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n785\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n786\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n787\t                    })\n788\t\n789\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n790\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n791\t                await        # Update intent status to failed\n800\t                store = get_store()\n801\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n802\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n803\t                    \"failed\",\n804\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n805\t                )\n806\t\n807\t                return {\n808\t                    \"intent_id\": synth_start = timings.clock()\n853\t            try:\n854\t                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n855\t            except Exception as synth_e:\n856\t                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n857\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n858\t                logger.error(\n859\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n860\t                )\n861\t\n862\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n863\t                await \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n segmentation prompt. LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\n    - Reduced max_tokens (128 vs 256) for faster generation\n    - Dedicated 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._router_zai_client = None  # Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n        return self._router_zai_client\n\n    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n        import hashlib\n        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n\n        if cache_key in _ROUTER_CACHE:\n            classifications, timestamp = _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n            age = time.time() - timestamp\n            if age < _CACHE_TTL:\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache HIT for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n                return classifications\n            else:\n                # Remove expired entry\n                del _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache EXPIRED for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]}\")\n\n        return None\n\n    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n        # Prune cache if at capacity\n        if len(_ROUTER_CACHE) >= _MAX_CACHE_SIZE:\n            # Remove oldest entry (first in dict)\n            oldest_key = next(iter(_ROUTER_CACHE))\n            del _ROUTER_CACHE[oldest_key]\n            logger.info(f\"Router cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n\n        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n        _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key] = (classifications, time.time())\n        logger.info(f\"Router cache STORED utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (cache size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n\n        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n\n        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._load_router_prompt()\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n        if cached_result is not None:\n            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n            empty_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": True,\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n                \"process_ms\": 0,\n                \"total_ms\": 0,\n                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n            }\n            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            # Measure total classification time\n            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n\n            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Build user message\n            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n                temperature=0.0,\n                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n            )\n            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n\n            # Extract content and timing from response\n            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Measure JSON parsing time\n            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n            try:\n                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n                raise e\n            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure classification processing time\n            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n\n            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n\n            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            inference_str = f\"{timing_inference_ms:.2f}\" if timing_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n                f\"proxy_inference_ms={inference_str} \"\n                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n\n            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n            timing_breakdown = {\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(timing_inference_ms, 2) if timing_inference_ms is not None else None,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n            }\n\n            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n\n            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                    return classifications, _\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n            try:\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update          status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n         target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n                fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = \"\"\"\n50\t\n51\t    def __init__(self):\n52\t        self._source_executors = {\n53\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n54\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS:
